name: DIRA (Deep Realization Assessment)
description: Uncovers hidden narratives, limiting beliefs, emotional triggers, and personal agency.

questions.text: When you think about your current life situation, what is the primary story or narrative you tell yourself about why things are the way they are?
options: Open-ended response

questions.text: Identify a recurring challenge in your life. What is the underlying 'limiting belief' you hold about yourself or others regarding this challenge?
options: Open-ended response

questions.text: When you encounter a sudden setback, what is your immediate emotional or cognitive response?
options: I assume it's my fault and feel defeated
I get frustrated and look for someone or something to blame
I pause to analyze what happened and look for lessons
I try to ignore it and move on quickly

questions.text: Recall a recent time you felt triggered or highly reactive. What was the unspoken expectation or rule that you felt was violated?
options: Open-ended response

questions.text: To what extent do you feel in control of the direction and outcomes of your life?
options: Almost completely in control
Mostly in control, but influenced by external factors
Very little control, feeling swept by circumstances
No control at all

questions.text: What is a 'should' or 'must' rule that you frequently apply to yourself (e.g., 'I must always be perfect', 'I should never show weakness')?
options: Open-ended response

questions.text: How easily can you shift your perspective when presented with new evidence that contradicts your initial stance?
options: Very easily; I enjoy updating my viewpoint
With some effort, once I verify the facts
Reluctantly, as it feels uncomfortable to be wrong
Hardly ever; I stick to my views

questions.text: Who or what do you find yourself blaming most often when things do not go according to your plans?
options: Open-ended response

questions.text: When someone gives you constructive feedback, what is your initial internal reaction?
options: I feel criticized and personally attacked
I feel defensive and immediately start explaining myself
I listen objectively and evaluate if it is helpful
I agree outwardly but ignore it internally

questions.text: What is a personal strength or resource you possess that you tend to undervalue or ignore?
options: Open-ended response

questions.text: How do you view failures or mistakes in your personal or professional life?
options: As proof of my inadequacy
As frustrating setbacks that shouldn't happen
As essential learning opportunities for growth
As minor events that don't matter much

questions.text: If you could change one fundamental way you interpret your daily experiences, what shift would make the biggest difference in your peace of mind?
options: Open-ended response

questions.text: How aligned do you feel with your core values and sense of purpose on a daily basis?
options: Fully aligned and actively living my purpose
Somewhat aligned, but frequently distracted
Rarely aligned, feeling disconnected
Not aligned at all, feeling lost

questions.text: Rate your overall level of optimism regarding your future on a scale from 1 to 10.
What is one commitment or action step you are willing to take today to reclaim your personal agency and rewrite your narrative?
options: Open-ended response
